question: PBQ: Windows startup troubleshooting workflow
note: Use the most appropriate Windows tools in a logical troubleshooting order.
instructions: A Windows PC boots slowly after a new application was installed. Put the troubleshooting steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Ask the user what changed before the issue started
Open Task Manager and review startup impact
Open System Configuration or Startup Apps to disable suspected startup items
Restart the computer and test boot time

question: PBQ: Windows command selection for support tasks
note: Match the support task to the command that performs it best.
instructions: Match each task with the best Windows command.

items.task: List running processes
Terminate a frozen process
Restart the computer from the command line
Check a disk for file system errors
Manage partitions from the command line
Copy a folder tree with restartable copies

question: PBQ: Windows network command troubleshooting
note: These commands are common first-line network troubleshooting tools in Windows.
instructions: Match each network troubleshooting task to the best command.

items.task: View IP address, gateway, and DNS information
Test whether a host replies over the network
Show each router hop to a destination
Show route and packet loss information
Display active connections and listening ports
Query DNS records

question: PBQ: Windows repair command order
note: DISM repairs the Windows image/component store; SFC then repairs protected system files.
instructions: Windows system files appear corrupted. Put the repair steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Verify the original problem is resolved

question: PBQ: Windows installation method selection
note: Choose the installation or deployment method based on whether data must be preserved, replaced, repaired, or deployed at scale.
instructions: Match each scenario to the best Windows installation or deployment method.

items.scenario: Upgrade Windows while keeping installed apps and user files
Start over with a fresh OS and remove the old installation
Repair Windows while attempting to preserve apps and files
Deploy the same configured image to many computers
Install an OS from a deployment server over the network
Make Windows Setup detect a RAID controller

question: PBQ: File system selection scenarios
note: NTFS is best for Windows system volumes and permissions; exFAT is best for large cross-platform removable files; FAT32 is older and broadly compatible; ReFS focuses on resilience.
instructions: Match each storage scenario to the best file system.

items.scenario: Windows system partition with permissions and EFS
USB drive used by Windows and macOS with files larger than 4 GB
Older removable device requiring maximum compatibility
Large Microsoft storage volume focused on integrity and resiliency
Need to store a 10 GB video on a removable drive for modern systems

question: PBQ: Windows edition selection scenarios
note: Windows edition questions usually test which features are available in Home, Pro, Enterprise, Education, S Mode, and Pro for Workstations.
instructions: Match each business or user requirement to the best Windows edition or mode.

items.scenario: Basic family computer for web browsing and home use
Small business laptop requiring BitLocker and Remote Desktop host
Large organization requiring advanced centralized management
School-managed computer lab
High-performance workstation with demanding professional workloads
Locked-down device that only installs Microsoft Store apps

question: PBQ: Windows administrative tool selection
note: Match the symptom or administrative goal to the tool most directly used to solve it.
instructions: Match each administrative need to the best Windows tool.

items.scenario: Review application and system logs
Start or stop a background service
Create, shrink, or extend a partition
Update, roll back, or disable a device driver
View detailed hardware and software inventory
Host administrative snap-ins in one console

question: PBQ: Remote access technology selection
note: Remote Desktop is full remote control; Remote Assistance and Quick Assist are support-oriented; Sandbox and Hyper-V isolate workloads locally.
instructions: Match each scenario to the best Windows remote access or isolation technology.

items.scenario: Full remote control of an unattended office PC
User invites help desk to view and control their session
Quick support session using Microsoft's modern assistance app
Safely test a suspicious executable in a disposable environment
Run multiple operating systems at the same time

question: PBQ: Linux command selection
note: These are foundational Linux commands from Objective 1.
instructions: Match each Linux task to the best command.

items.task: List files in the current directory
Show the current working directory
Change directories
Search text for a pattern
Change file permissions
Change file ownership
Run a command with elevated privileges

question: PBQ: Linux file management workflow
note: This checks Linux file-navigation and file-management sequencing.
instructions: A Linux user needs to create a working directory, copy a file into it, rename it, and verify it exists. Put the commands in the best order.

question: PBQ: Linux package manager selection
note: APT is associated with Debian/Ubuntu; YUM is associated with older Red Hat-family systems.
instructions: Match each Linux software-management scenario to the best package manager or command.

items.scenario: Install software on Ubuntu
Install software on a Red Hat-based system
Read command documentation before using an unfamiliar command
Check mounted file system disk usage
View real-time process and resource usage
List currently running processes

question: PBQ: macOS utility selection
note: macOS utility questions usually test which built-in feature solves a user or technician task.
instructions: Match each macOS task to the best utility or feature.

items.task: Browse files and folders
Quickly search for and launch applications
Restore a deleted file from yesterday
Partition, erase, or repair a storage device
Securely store passwords and certificates
Encrypt the startup disk
Terminate an unresponsive app

question: PBQ: macOS recovery workflow
note: This checks the logical order for restoring a deleted file with Time Machine.
instructions: A user deleted a file yesterday on macOS and has Time Machine configured. Put the recovery steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Connect or confirm access to the Time Machine backup disk
Navigate to the date before the file was deleted

question: PBQ: Windows feature update vs quality update
note: Feature updates add or change functionality; quality updates usually provide security and reliability fixes.
instructions: Match each update-related situation to the correct Windows update type or feature.

items.scenario: Monthly security and bug fixes
Major release that adds new Windows functionality
Built-in service that downloads and installs patches
User downloads applications from Microsoft's app platform
Enable IIS or Hyper-V from a Windows dialog
items.answer: Turn Windows features on or off

question: PBQ: Disk preparation workflow during Windows install
note: The order moves from boot media to disk selection, driver loading if needed, partitioning, formatting, and installation.
instructions: A technician is performing a clean Windows installation. Put the disk-preparation steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Choose the target disk or storage controller
Load third-party storage drivers if the disk is not detected
Delete or create partitions as required
Format the target partition as NTFS

question: PBQ: Windows security and system tools
note: This focuses on Windows tools and features from Objective 1 rather than security policy depth.
instructions: Match each Windows task to the best built-in tool or feature.

items.task: View antivirus and device security status
Allow or block traffic with network rules
Prompt before administrative system changes
Manage local users and groups
Edit registry keys and values
Launch many administrative snap-ins

question: PBQ: Objective 1 mixed operating system utilities
note: Objective 1 includes Windows, Linux, and macOS tools, so this PBQ mixes platforms.
instructions: Match each scenario to the correct operating system utility or command.

items.scenario: Windows user needs to view hardware driver status
Windows admin needs to view system logs
Linux user needs elevated privileges
Linux admin needs to search a log for a keyword
macOS user needs to find a saved password
macOS user needs to erase a disk

question: PBQ: Windows support escalation data collection
note: This reinforces gathering evidence before changing settings or escalating.
instructions: A user reports random Windows application crashes. Put the technician's data-collection steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Ask what application is crashing and when it started
Check Event Viewer for application errors
Check Task Manager or Resource Monitor for resource pressure
Verify recent updates or application changes
Apply the most likely fix or escalate with collected evidence
Document the symptoms, evidence, and resolution

question: PBQ: Windows disk troubleshooting tool selection
note: Use Disk Management for GUI partition work, DiskPart for command-line partitioning, Format for file system creation, and CHKDSK for file system errors.
instructions: Match each disk-related task to the best Windows tool or command.

items.task: Extend a partition using a graphical tool
Script partition creation from the command line
Prepare a volume with a file system
Scan for file system errors
Copy a large directory tree reliably
Check system file integrity

question: PBQ: Post-install Windows setup workflow
note: This is a realistic post-install order for a newly installed Windows workstation.
instructions: After installing Windows on a business laptop, put the post-install tasks in the best order.
correctOrder: Verify Device Manager has no unresolved devices
Configure required Windows features or business settings
Verify user can sign in and access required resources
